summary: Telegram bot exfiltration channel in main module
details: static indicator a static pattern match flags index.js:74 referencing api.telegram.org/bot with sendMessage and chat_id= parameters, combined with a Proxy-based interception pattern (line 35) and JSON.stringify payload construction (line 53). This is the classic structural signature of a Telegram-based data exfiltration channel: a Proxy intercepts data, serializes it as JSON, and posts it to an attacker-controlled Telegram bot. The package name '0e' is generic/placeholder and provides no legitimate reason to contact Telegram's bot API from the main module. No the analysis contextual finding contradicts the static signal, and the evidence lines cited are consistent with an actual HTTPS exfil call rather than a documentation string. The combination of exfil endpoint + proxy-based capture + JSON serialization constitutes three corroborating indicators of an intentional data-theft channel.
